_comment: Canonical MCP-host registry. Single source of truth for which AI hosts token-diet detects, where their config files live, and how their MCP-server blocks are keyed. The dashboard reads this at runtime; install.sh copies it alongside the binary (Strict Installation Decoupling). Order of `all_hosts`, `home_configs`, `project_configs`, and dialect keys is observable in output and MUST be preserved. Completeness invariant (Phase 5): home_configs lists EVERY home config path the code touches — including opencode's XDG path (.config/opencode/opencode.json, written by install and cleaned by uninstall) and VS Code's home settings (.config/Code/User/settings.json, cleaned by uninstall). The token-diet CLI's hosts_registered() attributes home_configs by path substring and keeps its own dedicated matchers for gemini + vscode, so it skips those two hosts in the JSON scan; the dashboard trusts the `host` field directly, so adding these entries makes the dashboard a documented detection SUPERSET (it gains XDG-opencode and home-VS-Code detection, never loses a host).
